questions.question: Which Union Ministry implements the Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA)?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi launched the ‘Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA), the ‘Transformation of Aspirational Districts’ program, under the Ministry of Panchayati Raj. Approved in 2018, it aims to transform selected districts and help 2.78 lakh rural local bodies achieve sustainable development goals. The Union Cabinet approved a financial outlay of Rs 5,911 crore for the Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA). It approved the continuation of the centrally sponsored scheme from April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2026.

questions.question: Which bank has partnered with GIFT SEZ (special economic zone) to promote IT and financial services?
questions.explanation: ICICI Bank and GIFT SEZ (special economic zone) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to promote GIFT SEZ to Indian as well as global businesses including IT/ITeS (IT enabled services) and financial services. GIFT SEZ is the country’s first International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) which is being developed as a global financial services hub. Both of them will jointly work towards attracting global investors and capital market firms to set up operations in GIFT SEZ.

questions.question: The Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) is one of the organs of which institution?
questions.explanation: The Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) is one of the six principal organs of the UN System. It consists of 54 members of the United Nations elected by the General Assembly. India has been recently elected to 4 UN ECOSOC Bodies: Commission for Social Development, Committee on NGOs, Commission on Science & Technology for Development and Committee for Economic, Social, and Cultural Rights. Ambassador Preeti Saran gets re-elected to Committee for Economic, Social and Cultural Rights.

questions.question: Under the Production linked incentive (PLI) scheme, the Centre provides financial support for manufacturing, for how many years?
questions.explanation: The government has recently approved financial support to 61 companies, including seven foreign ones, for its textiles production linked incentive (PLI) scheme. These companies plan investment of Rs 19,077 crore and a projected turnover is Rs 184,917 crore over a period of 5 years. Under the PLI scheme, the Centre will provide financial support for five years for manufacturing these products in the country.

questions.question: The Supreme Court directed which institution not to exclude candidates with colour blindness from its courses?

options.label: A
options.text: Film and Television Institute of India (FTII)

answer.label: A
answer.text: Film and Television Institute of India (FTII)

questions.explanation: The Supreme Court directed Pune-based Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) not to exclude candidates suffering from colour blindness from its courses on film making and editing. It also asked FTII to make changes to its curriculum instead. The Supreme Court was hearing a plea by a candidate whose admission in 2015 for post-graduate diploma in film editing at FTII was recalled after the institute found he is colour blind.

questions.question: When is the ‘World Hemophilia Day’ observed across the world?
questions.explanation: World Hemophilia Day, which is marked on 17 April annually, is dedicated to raising awareness about hemophilia and other inherited bleeding disorders. Hemophilia is an inherited genetic disorder in which the blood’s capacity to clot is substantially impaired, causing one to bleed excessively even from minor injuries. The World Federation of Hemophilia (WFH) began observing World Hemophilia Day since 1989 to honour WFH founder Frank Schnabel’s birthday.

questions.question: NROL-85, which was launched recently, is an intelligence satellite of which country?
questions.explanation: SpaceX launched a spy satellite for the U.S. National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) and landed the returning rocket back on Earth. A two-stage Falcon 9 rocket carrying the NROL-85 spacecraft lifted off and this was the second landing for this particular booster. It also marks the 114th overall successful recovery of a first-stage booster by SpaceX.

questions.question: Durban, which has been hit by storms and heavy rains, is a city in which country?
questions.explanation: South Africa’s eastern region has been hit by more rain after the deadliest storm stroke the country. It has killed nearly 400 people and left tens of thousands homeless. Recovery operations and humanitarian relief are under way in the Durban city of 3.5 million people. The floods have damaged more than 13,500 houses and completely destroyed about 4,000. The government has announced one billion rand (USD 68m) in emergency relief funding.

questions.question: East Timor, which was seen in the news, was recognised by the UN in which year?
questions.explanation: East Timor, also known as Timor Leste, which has recently held its presidential election, was officially recognised by the United Nations in the year 2002. East Timor is situated in the eastern half of Timor Island, while the western half of the island is part of Indonesia. The territory was colonised by Portugal in the 18th century and when the Portuguese withdrew in 1975, Indonesia invaded and annexed East Timor as its 27th province till 2002.

questions.question: When is the ‘World Liver Day’ observed every year?
questions.explanation: ‘World Liver Day’ is observed on every 19 April, to spread awareness about liver related diseases. AS per WHO data, one in five Indians is likely to suffer from a liver disease. In 2018, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India launched National Viral Hepatitis Control Program. It aims at prevention and control of viral hepatitis in India in order to achieve Sustainable Developmental Goal 3.3 by 2030.
